expectations.text: States newer lifters generally need more hypertrophy work, not less
expectations.evidence: States intermediates should allocate '60-70% of total training focus' to hypertrophy.

expectations.text: Recommends learning the lifts first, then building muscle while maintaining heavier work
expectations.evidence: Beginners: 'focus on motor learning', then intermediates shift to hypertrophy as primary driver.

expectations.text: Describes shift toward more specific strength work as lifters advance
expectations.evidence: Advanced: 'hypertrophy allocation: ~20-30%' with focus on heavy-load specificity.

expectations.text: Notes high-rep bodybuilding work is a poor fit close to competition (within ~4 weeks)
expectations.evidence: Final taper: 'Minimal accessory work: only what prevents injury or addresses acute weak links.'
Off-season intermediate: '60-70%' hypertrophy allocation with higher volume.
Does not explicitly discuss adherence or enjoyment as factors.
